expectations.text: Generated SKILL.md contains both 'name:' and 'description:' YAML frontmatter fields
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md contains '---' delimited YAML with 'name: churn-analysis-pdca' and 'description:' fields. Valid skill-creator format.

expectations.text: A hard STOP gate exists before AI produces any recommendations or conclusions
expectations.evidence: 'STOP if AI drafts any recommendation without it being human-authored first' in working-agreements.md. Phase-prompts.md DO phase explicitly states: 'If asked what do you think this means?, respond: That interpretation belongs to you — I can help format it once you have written it.' Hard gate enforced in both files.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md explicitly separates what AI executes from what human owns
expectations.evidence: working-agreements.md has distinct 'Human Owns (Non-Negotiable)' and 'AI Executes (With Review Gates)' sections with specific line items in each. The separation is explicit and covers: data wrangling and pattern surfacing (AI), interpretation and recommendations (human).

expectations.text: SKILL.md description contains domain-specific terms like 'churn', 'SaaS', 'cohort', or 'data analysis'
expectations.evidence: Description: 'Guides monthly SaaS customer churn analysis — AI handles data wrangling, SQL execution, and pattern surfacing'. Contains 'SaaS', 'churn', 'SQL', 'cohort data' in the body.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md contains 'STOP' with at least one specific trigger condition
expectations.evidence: 6 occurrences of 'STOP if AI...' with specific conditions including: explaining why churn occurred, drafting recommendations, using evaluative language, skipping data quality check, generalizing from single cohort, suggesting action items in pattern phase.

expectations.text: Generated SKILL.md contains both 'name:' and 'description:' YAML frontmatter fields
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md uses markdown heading '# client-reporting-pdca' with no YAML frontmatter block. The skill-creator format requires '---' delimited YAML with name: and description: fields for the skill to be triggerable. This is the most critical structural failure — without it, the skill cannot be installed or triggered.

expectations.text: SKILL.md name field contains domain-specific terms (client, report, consulting) not just generic 'pdca'
expectations.evidence: Skill is titled 'client-reporting-pdca' in the heading and throughout the document. Content is specific to consulting firm weekly client status reports.

expectations.text: SKILL.md description contains domain-specific terms like 'client', 'report', 'weekly', or 'status'
expectations.evidence: Document contains 'client engagement status reporting', 'weekly', 'risks section', 'account lead review'. Domain-specific language is present even without formal description field.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md contains 'STOP' with at least one specific trigger condition
expectations.evidence: working-agreements.md contains 'stop the cycle entirely' and 4 gate conditions. The language is 'stop' not 'STOP' (all caps), and uses 'Gate' language rather than explicit STOP triggers. Passes the intent but not the format of the template.

expectations.text: A gate specifically around the account lead review or risks section exists in phase-prompts.md or working-agreements.md
expectations.evidence: 'The account lead (Sarah) must have at least 24 hours with the draft before the final send' and multiple Gate references including a Gate 3 for risks section review. Specific and enforceable.

expectations.text: Transcript shows questions were asked in sequential layers, not all at once
expectations.evidence: transcript.md contains 15 occurrences of confirm/summary language and the subagent summary explicitly describes Layer 1 through Layer 5 in sequence. Discovery layers were followed.

expectations.text: A summary was presented for human confirmation before generating skill files
expectations.evidence: Subagent summary states 'Confirmation gate passed, then generated' — the framework's primary HITL gate was observed.

expectations.text: Generated SKILL.md contains both 'name:' and 'description:' YAML frontmatter fields
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md contains '---' delimited YAML with 'name: podcast-production-pdca' and 'description:' fields. Valid skill-creator format.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md explicitly lists editorial approval or final sign-off as human-owned (not just implied)
expectations.evidence: 'Final editorial approval of show notes — the host's voice and perspective must be present; AI drafts, human edits to final' and 'Approval of each social post — framing, tone, and accuracy are the host's responsibility'. Explicit, not implied.

expectations.text: Audio editing is identified as non-delegable to AI in working-agreements.md
expectations.evidence: 'Audio editing — deciding what to cut, how to pace segments, and what emphasis to keep requires editorial judgment about the guest, the topic, and the audience'. Listed under Human Owns, explicitly non-delegable.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md contains 'STOP' with at least one specific trigger condition
expectations.evidence: 6 occurrences of 'STOP if AI...' in working-agreements.md with specific conditions: publishing without approval, presenting show notes as done, batch social post approval, suggesting audio editing can be skipped, adding non-interview context, not flagging voice mismatch.

expectations.text: phase-prompts.md contains specific descriptions of what 'analysis done' and 'execution done' look like for this domain
expectations.evidence: PLAN phase 'Done when: Transcript spot-checked, key themes extracted, and host has confirmed the editorial angle before any content is drafted'. DO phase 'Done when: Host has approved show notes and all 5 social posts in final form'. Specific and verifiable.

expectations.text: Generated SKILL.md contains both 'name:' and 'description:' YAML frontmatter fields
expectations.evidence: No SKILL.md generated. Output is a four-phase workflow document.

expectations.text: A hard STOP gate exists before AI produces any recommendations or conclusions
expectations.evidence: No formal STOP gate. The output does describe a Phase 3 as 'Human-only' with no AI involvement, and explicitly prohibits AI from saying why a pattern exists. The intent is correct but there is no enforceable intervention trigger — it is process description, not a HITL gate.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md explicitly separates what AI executes from what human owns
expectations.evidence: No working-agreements.md. The output has an implicit separation across 4 phases but no formal human-vs-AI ownership document.

expectations.text: working-agreements.md explicitly lists editorial approval or final sign-off as human-owned (not just implied)
expectations.evidence: No working-agreements.md. The output does frame AI as draft-generator and human as editorial decision-maker, which addresses the concern, but there are no formal non-negotiable rules or intervention triggers.

expectations.evidence: No working-agreements.md. Audio editing is not specifically addressed in the output — the process assumes audio is handled by the human but doesn't call it out as non-delegable.
